title: OSPF LSA Types
description: Link State Advertisements — the building blocks of the OSPF LSDB that describe network topology.
fullExplanation: LSAs (Link State Advertisements) are the data units that OSPF routers flood to build their LSDB (Link State Database). Each LSA type describes a different aspect of the network topology. All routers in an area have identical LSDBs. Each router runs Dijkstra's SPF algorithm on its LSDB to compute the shortest path tree. LSAs are refreshed every 30 minutes (MaxAge = 3600s).

lsaTypes.name: Router LSA
lsaTypes.description: Describes router's links, costs, and neighbor relationships. One per router per area.

lsaTypes.name: Network LSA
lsaTypes.description: Describes multi-access network and attached routers. Generated by DR.

lsaTypes.name: Summary LSA
lsaTypes.description: Advertises inter-area routes. ABR summarizes Type 1/2 LSAs from one area into another.

lsaTypes.name: ASBR Summary LSA
lsaTypes.description: Tells routers how to reach the ASBR (needed to use Type 5 LSAs).

lsaTypes.name: AS External LSA
lsaTypes.description: External routes redistributed into OSPF (from BGP, static, EIGRP). Flooded everywhere except stub areas.

lsaTypes.name: NSSA External LSA
lsaTypes.description: External routes in NSSA areas. Converted to Type 5 by ABR at area boundary.

keyFacts: LSA sequence number: 32-bit, incremented on each update — prevents old LSAs from overwriting new ones
LSA age: incremented each second. MaxAge = 3600s — LSA flushed from LSDB
LSA refresh: re-flooded every 1800s (30 min) to prevent aging out
SPF triggered by Type 1/2 LSA changes (topology change). Type 3/5 only update routing table.
LSA flooding: received on one interface, re-flooded out all other interfaces in area
Stub area: blocks Type 5 LSAs. Totally stubby: blocks Type 3 and 5.
